back_translation: He entered into the synagogue once again, and there was a man there, his hand was withered.
They watched Him closely whether He would heal that man on the Sabbath day or not, in order to find a reason to accuse Him.
He then said to the man who had the withered hand: 'Get up and stand here in the middle.'
Then He said to them: 'On the Sabbath day, what is permitted — doing good or doing evil, saving a life or killing?' But they remained silent.
He looked around with anger, was grieved because of the hardness of their hearts, then said to that man: 'Stretch out your hand.' He stretched it out, and his hand returned to normal.
The Pharisees then went out immediately, and together with the Herodians took counsel together how they might destroy Him.
Jesus went out with His disciples to the Sea of Galilee, and a great crowd from the region of Galilee and from the region of Judea followed.

And from the city of Jerusalem, from the region of Idumea, from the other side of the Jordan River, and from the regions of the cities Tyre and Sidon, a great crowd having heard what He had done came to Him.
He commanded His disciples to prepare a small boat to be ready for Him at all times, because of the crowd, so that they would not press upon Him.
Because He had healed many people, so that those who had various diseases pressed in to touch Him.
Every time the unclean spirits saw Him, they would fall down before His presence and cry out: 'You are the Son of God.'
But He sternly charged them not to make known who He was.
Then He went up on the mountain, called those whom He desired to come to Him, and they came to Him.
He appointed twelve disciples [whom He also called apostles], so that they would be with Him, and so that He would send them out to proclaim.
and to have authority to cast out demons.
He appointed the twelve disciples, namely: Simon (to whom He gave the name Peter)
James son of Zebedee, and John the younger brother of James (He gave them both the name 'Boanerges,' which means 'Sons of Thunder')
Andrew, Philip, Bartholomew, Matthew, Thomas, James son of Alphaeus, Thaddaeus, and Simon the Cananaean.
and Judas Iscariot, the one who later betrayed Him.
Then He returned into the house, and the crowd gathered together again, so that He and the disciples did not even have time to eat food.
When His relatives heard this, they went out to take Him back, because people had said that He had lost His mind.
And the scribes who came down from the city of Jerusalem said: 'He is possessed by Beelzebul,' and: 'He casts out demons by the power of the chief of demons.'
He then called them to come, and said to them in a parable: 'How can Satan cast out Satan?'
If any kingdom is divided as an adversary against itself, that kingdom cannot stand.
And if any family is divided as an adversary against itself, that family will not be able to stand.
And if Satan rises up to fight against himself and is divided, he cannot stand, but has reached his end.
But no one can enter into the house of a strong man and plunder his possessions unless he first binds the strong man, and then he can plunder his house.
I tell you the truth: the sons of men will be forgiven in all things, both sins and all blasphemies, whatever they blaspheme.
But whoever blasphemes the Holy Spirit will not be forgiven forever, but is guilty of an eternal sin.
(He said this) because they had said: 'He has an unclean spirit.'
Then His mother and His younger brothers arrived, stood outside, and sent someone in to call Him.
A crowd was sitting around Him, so they said to Him: 'Behold, your mother and your younger brothers are outside, looking for you.'
He answered them: 'Who is my mother, and who are my brothers?'
He looked at those who sat around Him, then said: 'Behold, here is my mother and my brothers.'
Because whoever does the will of God, that one is my brother, my sister, and my mother.
